title: rosa glauca subsp. pseudomarsica
intro: Rosa glauca subsp. pseudomarsica, also known as the 'Rose of Sharon', is a plant species within the Rosa glauca complex, characterized by its distinctive blue-gray foliage and fragrant white flowers. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its purported anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting potential antiviral effects. Rosa glauca subsp. pseudomarsica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically validated.
